task.title: Configuring user-defined network interface names by using systemd link files

li.p: You must meet one of these conditions: NetworkManager does not manage this interface, or the corresponding connection profile uses the keyfile format.

context.p: You can use systemd link files to implement custom network interface names that reflect your organization’s requirements.

step.cmd: Identify the network interface that you want to rename:

info.codeblock: # ip link show ... enp1s0: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1500 qdisc fq_codel state UP mode DEFAULT group default qlen 1000 link/ether 00:00:5e:00:53:1a brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff ...
info.p: Record the MAC address of the interface.

step.cmd: If it does not already exist, create the /etc/systemd/network/ directory:
For each interface that you want to rename, create a 70-*.link file in the /etc/systemd/network/ directory with the following content:

note.p: Use a file name with a 70- prefix to keep the file names consistent with the udev rules-based solution.

info.p: For example, create the /etc/systemd/network/70-provider0.link file with the following content to rename the interface with MAC address 00:00:5e:00:53:1a to provider0:

step.cmd: Optional: Regenerate the initrd RAM disk image:

info.p: You require this step only if you need networking capabilities in the RAM disk. For example, this is the case if the root file system is stored on a network device, such as iSCSI.

step.cmd: Identify which NetworkManager connection profile uses the interface that you want to rename:

info.codeblock: # nmcli -f device,name connection show DEVICE NAME enp1s0 example_profile ...

step.cmd: Unset the connection.interface-name property in the connection profile:
Temporarily, configure the connection profile to match both the new and the previous interface name:

info.codeblock: # nmcli connection modify example_profile match.interface-name "provider0 enp1s0"

step.cmd: Verify that the device with the MAC address that you specified in the link file has been renamed to provider0:

info.codeblock: # ip link show provider0: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1500 qdisc mq state UP mode DEFAULT group default qlen 1000 link/ether 00:00:5e:00:53:1a brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff ...

step.cmd: Configure the connection profile to match only the new interface name:

info.p: You have now removed the old interface name from the connection profile.
